text:
Once upon a time, atop a hill shrouded in mist, there lived a lone raven. This bird wasn't just any raven; she was wise, ancient, and had witnessed countless winters. Every morning, she perched on a gnarl-infested branch overlooking the village below. The villagers held various myths about her.

One said she was mourning the passing of a mate. Another claimed she knew of hidden treasures. Still, another believed she prophesied the fate of those who looked into her keen eyes.

One dreary morning, a young boy ventured up the slope. Trembling, he dared not look directly into her piercing gaze. Instead, he whispered a question about the stars.

"Who do you miss?" he asked.

A single wing beat slowly in answer.

"The one you lost..."

She tilted her head.

"The bright one..."

Her eyes twinkled like stars.

"The one you'll always keep?"

Silence.

The boy smiled. "Can you talk?"

"Not
